title: sedum telephium var. lugdunense
intro: Sedum telephium var. lugdunense, also known as Burdock-leaved or Burgundy Stonecrop, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sedum telephium var. lugdunense include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it can be used both internally and externally to reap its benefits.
